TECHNICAL_DOCS.DESCRIPTION: Use this documentation hub as the first stop before adding or changing a mini project.

EMPTY.TEXT: Clear the search or try another architecture, translation, or quality keyword.
EMPTY.TITLE: No documentation sections found

SEARCH.LABEL: Search
SEARCH.PLACEHOLDER: Search architecture, i18n, tests, or files

ITEMS.LAZY: Project screens are lazy-loaded from the project detail workspace.
ITEMS.ROUTES: Application routes send users directly into the admin dashboard and project detail views.
ITEMS.SHELL: Header, sidebar, footer, and the scrollable main area are handled by the shared admin shell.

ARCHITECTURE.SUMMARY: The app is organized around a fixed admin shell, feature routes, and standalone project components.
ARCHITECTURE.TITLE: Application architecture

ITEMS.KEYS: Visible text should use translation keys instead of hard-coded labels.
ITEMS.LENGTH: Longer Macedonian text must be checked in card, sidebar, and button layouts.
ITEMS.MACEDONIAN: Macedonian translations must be complete Cyrillic text.

I18N.SUMMARY: English and Macedonian translations stay paired so the catalog, project details, and mini apps remain consistent.
I18N.TITLE: Translations

ITEMS.COMPONENT: Each mini project lives in its own standalone component folder.
ITEMS.COVER: Every project needs a cover asset that works in catalog big, list, and detailed views.
ITEMS.REGISTRY: The registry defines order, route, metadata, tags, and project status.

PROJECT_FLOW.SUMMARY: A new project should follow the same folder, metadata, translation, and testing path.
PROJECT_FLOW.TITLE: Adding a project

ITEMS.BUILD: Run the production build and keep the task checklist updated.
ITEMS.E2E: Add Playwright coverage when a project introduces a new user workflow.
ITEMS.UNIT: Unit tests should cover the core state transitions and validation rules.

QUALITY.SUMMARY: Every project should be easy to verify with focused tests, build checks, and documentation updates.
QUALITY.TITLE: Quality checks

TECHNICAL_DOCS.TITLE: Technical Documentation

TIC_TAC_TOE.DESCRIPTION: A compact two-player grid game rebuilt as a standalone Angular component with signal-based state.

FEATURES.RESET: The board can be reset instantly after a win or a draw.
FEATURES.TURNS: Players alternate local turns between X and O.
FEATURES.WINNER: Rows, columns, diagonals, and draw states are detected automatically.

TIC_TAC_TOE.RESET_GAME_CONFIRM_TEXT: The current round is still active. Restarting will clear this board.
TIC_TAC_TOE.RESET_SCORE_CONFIRM_TEXT: This will clear X wins, O wins, draws, and the current board.
TIC_TAC_TOE.ROUND_DRAW_TEXT: No winner this round. Start a new round when you are ready.
TIC_TAC_TOE.TITLE: Interactive Tic Tac Toe

FEATURES.ARITHMETIC: Addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, decimals, percent, and sign toggle.
FEATURES.ERRORS: Division by zero is handled with a visible error state.
FEATURES.KEYBOARD: Keyboard shortcuts work for digits, operators, Enter, Escape, decimal, and percent.

JAVASCRIPT_QUIZ.INTRO_TEXT: Each round draws {{count}} random questions from a bank of {{total}}. You have {{seconds}} seconds for each question.
JAVASCRIPT_QUIZ.QUESTION: Question {{number}}

ASSIGNMENT.TEXT: In a classic non-strict browser script, what does console.log(y) print after var x = window.y = 5;?
ASSIGNMENT.EXPLANATION: The chained assignment writes 5 to window.y, and var x receives the same value.

RETURN_OBJECT.TEXT: What does a function return when return is followed by a line break and then an object literal?
RETURN_OBJECT.EXPLANATION: Automatic semicolon insertion ends the return statement at the line break, so the function returns undefined.

THIS_CONTEXT.TEXT: Why can a detached method return a different value than the same method called through its object?

OPTIONS.C: this is determined by the call site

THIS_CONTEXT.EXPLANATION: For a regular function, the value of this depends on how the function is called, not where it was defined.

REVERSE_ARRAY.TEXT: After const arr2 = arr1.reverse(), what relationship is true?

OPTIONS.B: Both variables reference the same reversed array
OPTIONS.D: arr2 is converted to a string

REVERSE_ARRAY.EXPLANATION: reverse() mutates the original array and returns a reference to that same array.

CLOSURE.TEXT: Which statement best defines a JavaScript closure?

OPTIONS.D: A function that retains access to its lexical outer scope

CLOSURE.EXPLANATION: A closure keeps access to bindings from its lexical environment even after the outer function has finished.

FIBONACCI.EXPLANATION: Each Fibonacci number is the sum of the previous two numbers, so 8 + 13 equals 21.

VARIABLE_NAME.TEXT: Which name is a valid JavaScript variable identifier?
VARIABLE_NAME.EXPLANATION: Identifiers may start with a letter, underscore, or dollar sign, and they cannot be reserved words.

NULL_UNDEFINED.TEXT: Which statement correctly compares null and undefined?

OPTIONS.A: They are the same value and type
OPTIONS.B: Both represent missing values, but they are different types

NULL_UNDEFINED.EXPLANATION: Both are nullish values, but null is explicitly assigned while undefined commonly means no value was assigned.

REMAINDER.EXPLANATION: The remainder operator returns what remains after integer division, which is 5.

PUSH_POP.TEXT: Which statement about push() and pop() is correct?

OPTIONS.D: push adds to the end and pop removes from the end

PUSH_POP.EXPLANATION: push() appends one or more items, while pop() removes and returns the last item.

SHIFT_UNSHIFT.TEXT: What do shift() and unshift() do?

OPTIONS.B: They remove from and add to the start of an array

SHIFT_UNSHIFT.EXPLANATION: shift() removes the first item and unshift() adds items to the beginning.

SLICE.TEXT: What is a key property of Array.prototype.slice()?

OPTIONS.C: It returns a selected shallow copy without mutating the source

SLICE.EXPLANATION: slice() returns a shallow copy of the selected range and leaves the original array unchanged.

SPLICE.TEXT: Which array method can remove or insert items by mutating the original array?
SPLICE.EXPLANATION: splice() changes the original array by removing, replacing, or inserting elements.

CALL_APPLY.TEXT: What is the traditional difference between call() and apply()?

OPTIONS.D: call accepts separate arguments, while apply accepts an array-like argument list

CALL_APPLY.EXPLANATION: Both invoke a function with an explicit this value; their classic difference is how arguments are supplied.

MISSING_PROPERTY.TEXT: What is returned when reading an object property that does not exist?
MISSING_PROPERTY.EXPLANATION: Reading a missing property normally evaluates to undefined.

JSON_SYNTAX.TEXT: Which rule is required by valid JSON?

OPTIONS.C: Object property names use double quotes

JSON_SYNTAX.EXPLANATION: JSON requires double-quoted property names and does not support functions, comments, or trailing commas.

FUNCTION_EXPRESSION.TEXT: What usually happens when a var-assigned function expression is called before its assignment executes?

OPTIONS.A: A TypeError occurs because the variable is undefined

FUNCTION_EXPRESSION.EXPLANATION: The var declaration is hoisted as undefined, but the function value is assigned only when execution reaches the assignment.

FIRST_CLASS_FUNCTIONS.TEXT: What does it mean that JavaScript has first-class functions?

OPTIONS.D: Functions can be stored, passed, and returned like other values

FIRST_CLASS_FUNCTIONS.EXPLANATION: Functions are values, so they can be assigned to variables, passed as arguments, and returned from other functions.

PROTOTYPE_CHAIN.TEXT: Where does JavaScript continue looking when a property is not found directly on an object?
PROTOTYPE_CHAIN.EXPLANATION: Property lookup follows the prototype chain until the property is found or the chain ends.

SPREAD_ARRAY.TEXT: What does array spread syntax commonly do in [...first, ...second]?

OPTIONS.C: Creates a new array containing values from both sources

SPREAD_ARRAY.EXPLANATION: Spread expands iterable values into a new array and does not mutate the source arrays by itself.

REST_PARAMETERS.TEXT: What does a rest parameter such as ...values collect?
REST_PARAMETERS.EXPLANATION: A rest parameter gathers the remaining function arguments into a real array.

OPTIONS.D: Extracting values from arrays or properties from objects into variables

DESTRUCTURING.EXPLANATION: Destructuring provides concise syntax for unpacking array values and object properties.

MAP.TEXT: What kind of collection is a JavaScript Map?

OPTIONS.B: A key-value collection whose keys can be any value

MAP.EXPLANATION: Map stores key-value pairs and permits values of any type as keys.

SET.TEXT: What is the defining behavior of a JavaScript Set?
SET.EXPLANATION: Set is a collection in which each stored value can occur only once.

VAR_HOISTING.TEXT: What value is observed when a var variable is read before its assignment in the same scope?
VAR_HOISTING.EXPLANATION: The var declaration is hoisted and initialized with undefined; the later assignment is not hoisted.

STRICT_MODE.TEXT: What is one benefit of JavaScript strict mode?

OPTIONS.D: It turns some silent mistakes, such as accidental globals, into errors

STRICT_MODE.EXPLANATION: Strict mode applies safer semantics and reports several error-prone operations instead of silently accepting them.

PROJECT.PLACEHOLDER: The real Angular component for this project will live here.

MOVIE_SEARCH.DESCRIPTION: A compact movie explorer that simulates API loading, search, filters, pagination, and selected movie details.
MOVIE_SEARCH.EMPTY_TEXT: Clear the search or choose another genre to show movies again.
MOVIE_SEARCH.ERROR_TEXT: The simulated movie API request failed. Retry to reload the local movie data.

FEATURES.DETAILS: Selecting a movie updates a focused detail panel without changing routes.
FEATURES.FILTERS: Search and genre filters work together with paginated result cards.
FEATURES.STATES: Loading, empty, and error states are available without requiring an external API key.

MOVIE_SEARCH.TITLE: Movie Search

REST_COUNTRIES.DESCRIPTION: A REST Countries style explorer with simulated API loading, region filters, selected details, and persistent favorites.
REST_COUNTRIES.EMPTY_TEXT: Clear the search or select another region to show countries again.
REST_COUNTRIES.ERROR_TEXT: The simulated countries API request failed. Retry to reload the local country data.

FEATURES.DETAILS: Selecting a country shows capital, population, area, currency, languages, and borders.
FEATURES.FAVORITES: Favorite country codes are persisted in LocalStorage.
FEATURES.FILTERS: Search and region filters model the REST Countries API browsing workflow.

REST_COUNTRIES.TITLE: REST Countries

ERRORS.AMOUNT_POSITIVE: Enter an amount greater than zero.

CURRENCY_CONVERTER.ERROR_TEXT: The simulated rates API request failed. Retry to reload the local exchange rates.

FEATURES.REFRESH: Refresh and stale states model the lifecycle of external exchange-rate data.
FEATURES.SWAP: The source and target currencies can be swapped instantly.
FEATURES.VALIDATION: The amount field protects the conversion from invalid values.

CURRENCY_CONVERTER.TITLE: Currency Converter

QUOTES_API.DESCRIPTION: A quotes API style reader with loading, retry, favorites, LocalStorage persistence, and reduced-motion friendly typewriter text.

FEATURES.FAVORITES: Favorite quotes are persisted locally for quick reuse.
FEATURES.STATES: Loading, error, and retry flows model a small API integration.
